stem: A industrial engineering study measures the assembly time (seconds) for a sample of 25 factory workers. The sample mean is 326.3 sec with a sample standard deviation of 60.5 sec. However, 3 of the 25 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 22 observations have a mean of 315.7 sec and standard deviation of 37.8 sec. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 326.3, 95% CI = [301.3, 351.3]. Without outliers: mean = 315.7, 95% CI = [299.6, 331.8]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [299.6, 351.3]. Point estimate: 321.0 sec.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [299.6, 351.3].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
